title: What is Attention?
concept: Attention is a weighted similarity lookup mechanism: a query vector matches against a set of key vectors to retrieve values.

exercises.prompt: The Attention mechanism uses three main vectors to do its job. What are they?
exercises.explain: Attention is a lookup mechanism that uses Query, Key, and Value vectors to retrieve and focus on the most relevant information.

cards.title: Focus on What Matters
cards.body: When you look at a busy picture, your eyes don't focus on everything at once. You pay attention to the most important parts. AI does the exact same thing using math! Let's learn how it decides what is important.

cards.title: Defining Attention
cards.body: In AI, Attention is a mechanism that matches a 'Query' vector against a set of 'Key' vectors. The quality of this match decides how much of each corresponding 'Value' vector the AI should retrieve and focus on.

cards.title: The Library Lookup
cards.body:
Imagine you are in a library looking for a book on dinosaurs. 

Your question "Do you have books on dinosaurs?" is the Query. The catalog index tags on the shelves are the Keys. When you find a Key that matches your Query, you pull that book off the shelf. The actual pages inside the book you read are the Values!

cards.title: Attention in Action
cards.body:
When translating the sentence "The bank of the river," the word "bank" could mean a financial building or land next to water. 

The AI uses the Query ("bank") and checks the Keys of the surrounding words ("river"). Because "river" is a close semantic match for nature, the AI retrieves the Value for "land near water" instead of "money vault."

cards.title: Not a Slow Train!
cards.body:
Many people think AI models read sentences word-by-word in a strict sequence, like a train on a track. 

Actually, Attention allows the AI to look at all words at once and instantly connect related concepts, no matter how far apart they are in a sentence!

cards.title: Hard Lookup vs. Soft Attention
cards.body: Let's look at how Attention differs from traditional search. Traditional search wants an exact match, while Attention looks for semantic similarity.

right.text: Calculates similarity scores. It retrieves a weighted blend of the most relevant values.

left.text: Looks for exact database matches. If your key isn't a perfect match, you get nothing back.

exercises.prompt: What is the main role of the 'Key' vectors in the Attention mechanism?

options.text: They store the final output text directly.
They act as labels that are matched against the Query.
They delete irrelevant words from the computer's memory.
They are only used to count the number of words.

exercises.explain: Keys act as the search tags or labels. The Query is compared against these Keys to determine which Values are most relevant.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Attention retrieves a weighted mixture of values, rather than just choosing one single value.
exercises.explain: Attention calculates similarity scores (weights) to decide how much of each Value vector to combine. It is a soft, weighted blend rather than an all-or-nothing choice.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing piece of the Attention puzzle:
exercises.sentence: To find the right information, the AI compares the target ____ against all the Keys.
exercises.explain: The Query represents what you are currently looking for, which is then compared against all the Keys.

exercises.prompt: Which output shows an AI successfully using Attention keys to understand context?

options.text: Output A: The model translates 'bank' as a financial institution because it ignored the context key 'river'.
Output B: The model translates 'bank' as a riverbank because the Query matched the environmental context Keys.
Output C: The model randomly guesses a translation without comparing any keys.

exercises.explain: Only Output B shows a smart system using context keys (like 'chef' and 'kitchen') to correctly focus on cooking-related values instead of bank accounts.

exercises.prompt: You've reached the final challenge! Can you summarize the core mechanism of Attention in a single sentence?

options.text: A query vector is compared with key vectors to retrieve a weighted blend of value vectors.
It randomly selects one value vector from a list without checking the keys.
It discards the keys and queries to save memory and only stores values.
It acts as a hard filter that only allows exact, identical word matches.

exercises.explain: Attention acts like a soft search engine: it takes your Query, matches it mathematically against all available Keys, and retrieves a weighted mixture of the corresponding Values. This allows the model to focus on what matters most!
